description: Parser Differentials (PortSwigger Top 10 / 2025, #10 — joernchen). Two HTTP / URL / header / body parsers in the same pipeline (front-end + back-end, WAF + app, framework + library) disagree on the same input. Attacker crafts inputs that one parser sees as benign and the next sees as exploitable — auth bypass, cache key confusion, ACL bypass, smuggling, internal-path access.

url_path_normalisation_split.description: Front-end parser strips /./ and /../ before routing; back-end leaves them. Or front-end decodes %2e once, back-end twice. Result: /admin/../user reaches /admin on back-end.

probes.description: Request /public/../admin — observe whether front-end ACL (path-based) is bypassed at back-end. Compare to baseline GET /admin (which should be blocked).
Double-encode dot — /admin%252e%252e/secret — front-end sees admin%2e%2e/secret as opaque path, back-end re-decodes to /admin../secret which resolves to /secret.

header_name_differential.description: Header name parsing differs — duplicate Content-Length, mixed-case Transfer-Encoding, whitespace before colon, Unicode-equivalent names. Triggers smuggling, host-confusion, or WAF bypass.

probes.description: Send header `Conten‍t-Length: 100` (ZWJ inserted) — WAF strips the header (sees ContentLength), origin sees the standard length. Cross-test for smuggling.
Duplicate Host header — `Host: target.com` and `Host: internal.target.com`. Front-end honours first, back-end honours last (or vice versa). Internal routing reached via second Host.

content_type_charset_differential.description: Content-Type charset parsed differently — one parser uses UTF-8 (default), the next uses charset hint to interpret bytes. Crafted Latin-1 / ISO-8859-7 / UTF-7 body bypasses content filters.

probes.description: POST body with Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-7 — UTF-7-encoded `+ADw-script+AD4-` reaches XSS sink after one parser decodes.

json_parser_differential.description: JSON parser duplicates handle keys differently — first wins, last wins, both retained. Auth check sees `{role:user, role:admin}` as `user`, business logic sees `admin`.

probes.description: Submit JSON with duplicated authorisation key — first-wins parser at auth (sees user), last-wins parser at application (sees admin). Privilege escalation.
